title: Make a charge-card worker idempotent
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/non_idempotent_worker.ex` charges a customer and sends a receipt every time perform/1 runs. Oban retries on transient failures, so this worker will double-charge customers whenever Postgres or the mailer hiccups. Rewrite perform/1 so it first checks whether `invoice.charged_at` is already set — if so, return `:ok` without side effects. If not, call `Billing.charge_card/2`, then `Billing.mark_charged/2` to persist the charge id, then send the receipt. Assume `Billing.mark_charged/2` is available and returns `{:ok, invoice}`. Write the output to `lib/my_app/workers/invoice_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Straightforward idempotency fix once the problem is stated; the canonical pattern (check flag, then mark) is common across job queues.
research_citation: Medium: 'Keep Calm and Let Oban Handle Your Elixir Background Jobs' — 'Design your workers so that executing the same job multiple times does not produce adverse effects.'
